image: epithelioid cell type and prominent nucleoli (arrow).jpg
caption:
Microscopic description: The image displays a lesion characterized by a dense proliferation of spindle-shaped cells, arranged in a haphazard pattern. These cells exhibit moderate pleomorphism with irregular nuclei and prominent nucleoli. There is no clear interface with adjacent tissue or evidence of invasion. The cells are embedded within a fibrous stroma.

Key supporting features: Dense spindle cell proliferation; moderate nuclear pleomorphism; prominent nucleoli; fibrous stroma.

Likely interpretation: The lesion is consistent with a spindle cell neoplasm, such as a dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans or a low-grade fibromyxoid sarcoma, though the specific diagnosis cannot be definitively made without additional context or immunohistochemical staining. Further evaluation would be necessary to rule out other spindle cell tumors or reactive processes.
reference:
Pathologic diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma.
Microscopic findings: Epithelioid melanoma cells with prominent nucleoli are present.

image: low power shows a tumor in the subretinal area.jpg
caption:
Microscopic description: The image displays a lesion characterized by irregular, confluent sheets of neoplastic cells that infiltrate the dermis. These cells exhibit a mixture of spindle and epithelioid shapes, with moderate to marked pleomorphism. Nuclei are large, with distinct grooves and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are numerous, indicating high cell proliferation. Cytoplasm is variably eosinophilic, suggesting potential melanin pigmentation. The lesion shows a diffuse growth pattern, with no clear boundaries or nests.

Key supporting features: Irregular sheets, spindle and epithelioid cells, pleomorphism, large nuclei with grooves, prominent nucleoli, numerous mitotic figures, variable cytoplasmic pigmentation, diffuse growth pattern.

image: spindle A cell type has nuclear grooving (arrow).jpg
caption:
Microscopic description: The image displays a lesion characterized by irregular, confluent sheets of neoplastic cells that infiltrate the dermis. These cells exhibit a mixed morphology, with both spindle and epithelioid shapes. Nuclei are variably sized, with some showing nuclear grooves and pleomorphism. Mitotic figures are present, indicating a high proliferative activity. Cytoplasm is often vacuolated or eosinophilic, suggesting a possible reactive component.

Key supporting features: Irregular growth pattern; confluent sheets of mixed cell morphology; variable nuclear size and shape; presence of mitotic figures; vacuolated or eosinophilic cytoplasm.

Likely interpretation: The lesion is consistent with a poorly differentiated cutaneous sarcoma, possibly a malignant fibrous histiocytoma (MFH) or a dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans (DFS-P), given the mixed cell morphology and infiltrative growth pattern.
